title: lavandula officinalis var. pyrenaica
intro: Lavandula officinalis var. pyrenaica, also known as Pyrenean lavender, is a subspecies of the true lavender plant (Lavandula angustifolia) native to the Pyrenees Mountains. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and calming effects, which are often attributed to its high concentration of linalool and linalyl acetate. The health benefits of Lavandula officinalis var. pyrenaica include reducing anxiety and stress, promoting better sleep, and soothing skin irritations, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Lavandula officinalis var. pyrenaica is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, essential oils, and topical creams, which are often used to promote relaxation, reduce pain, and improve overall well-being.
